steps.title: Recognize the exponential form
steps.content: The function $$N(d) = 115  times (0.90)^d$$ is an exponential model with the standard form $$N(d) = a \times b^d$$, where $a = 115$ (the initial value) and $b = 0.90$ (the base).

steps.title: Interpret the multiplicative base
steps.content: In an exponential function, when the input increases by 1 unit, the output is multiplied by the base. Here, each unit increase in $d$ multiplies $N$ by 0.90. Since $0.90 = 1 - 0.10$, this represents a 10% decrease for each unit increase in $d$.

steps.title: Apply the depth units and identify the answer
steps.content:
The problem specifies that $d$ is measured in hundreds of meters.

Therefore, a one-unit increase in $d$ corresponds to a 100-meter increase in actual depth.

The model predicts that for every 100-meter increase in depth, the brachiopod species count decreases by 10%.

That matches choice D — D is correct.
